# Annotated Expo Playground

> An educational tool that helps students understand Expo/React Native code through AST-detected concept highlights and guided walkthroughs.

## What This Site Is

The Annotated Expo Playground is an interactive learning tool for beginner developers. Students paste React Native/Expo code into a code editor, and the system automatically:

1. Parses the code using AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) analysis
2. Detects programming concepts (hooks, components, styling patterns, etc.)
3. Highlights detected concepts directly in the code editor
4. Provides guided educational walkthroughs for each concept

## Content

The site contains tutorials for 18 React Native/Expo concepts organized into 4 categories:

### Basics
- Import statements
- Component functions
- JSX syntax
- Props
- Export default
- Programming basics
- View
- Text
- Image
- ScrollView
- Touchable components

### Hooks
- useState
- useEffect

### Styling
- StyleSheet
- Inline styles
- Flexbox layout

### Events
- onPress
- onChangeText

Each concept tutorial includes:
- Explanation of the concept for beginners
- Code examples in TypeScript/TSX
- Common patterns in Expo/React Native
- Links to official Expo documentation

## Languages

Content is available in:
- English (complete)
- Spanish (complete)

## Technology

Built with Next.js, CodeMirror 6, Babel AST analysis, and Snack SDK for live preview.
